name: Materials Science and Engineering
description: An interactive knowledge map of materials science spanning foundations, core concepts, material classes, processing, characterization, computation, AI, applications, and research frontiers.

children.name: Foundations
children.description: Mathematics, physics, chemistry, engineering, and computing that support the whole field.

children.name: Mathematics
children.description: The mathematical toolkit used across mechanics, transport, simulation, statistics, and materials data science.

children.name: Calculus
children.description: Differentiation, integration, multivariable calculus, and vector calculus for transport, field equations, and optimization.

children.name: Differential Equations
children.description: ODEs and PDEs for diffusion, heat flow, mechanics, kinetics, and field-based models.

children.name: Linear Algebra
children.description: Matrices, eigenvalues, tensors, and vector spaces for quantum mechanics, DFT, elasticity, and machine learning.

children.name: Probability and Statistics
children.description: Essential for uncertainty, experimental design, defect populations, reliability, and materials informatics.

children.name: Numerical Methods
children.description: Discretization, interpolation, optimization, and solvers for simulation and data analysis.

children.name: Physics
children.description: Physical laws governing structure, bonding, properties, transport, and collective behavior in materials.

children.name: Classical Mechanics
children.description: Force, momentum, deformation, and continuum response.

children.name: Electricity and Magnetism
children.description: Fields, charge transport, dielectric response, and magnetic behavior.

children.name: Thermodynamics and Statistical Mechanics
children.description: Energy, entropy, free energy, equilibrium, fluctuations, and ensembles.

children.name: Quantum Mechanics
children.description: The foundation for bonding, electronic structure, band theory, and spectroscopy.

children.name: Solid State and Condensed Matter Physics
children.description: Crystals, band structure, phonons, scattering, superconductivity, and correlated matter.

children.name: Chemistry
children.description: Chemical principles behind synthesis, bonding, phase stability, electrochemistry, and surfaces.

children.name: General Chemistry
children.description: Atomic structure, bonding, stoichiometry, and reaction fundamentals.

children.name: Physical Chemistry
children.description: Chemical thermodynamics, kinetics, and molecular interpretation of matter.

children.name: Inorganic Chemistry
children.description: Coordination, solid-state chemistry, and functional inorganic materials.

children.name: Organic and Polymer Chemistry
children.description: Macromolecules, polymerization, network formation, and molecular design.

children.name: Electrochemistry and Surface Chemistry
children.description: Redox reactions, interfaces, corrosion, batteries, and catalysis.

children.name: Engineering and Computing
children.description: Engineering literacy and computational fluency needed for experiments, modeling, and software-driven materials work.

children.name: Mechanics of Materials
children.description: Stress, strain, constitutive behavior, and failure concepts used throughout mechanical metallurgy and design.

children.name: Manufacturing Basics
children.description: How shaping, joining, and processing routes affect microstructure and performance.

children.name: Instrumentation and Laboratory Practice
children.description: Measurement, calibration, uncertainty, and safe experimentation.

children.name: Programming and Scientific Computing
children.description: Python, data workflows, automation, APIs, and reproducible research.

children.name: Core Principles
children.description: The common language of the field: processing, structure, defects, properties, and performance across length and time scales.

children.name: Processing-Structure-Properties-Performance
children.description: The foundational loop showing how processing choices create structure, which controls properties and real-world performance.

children.name: Composition and Microstructure
children.description: How chemistry, phases, grains, interfaces, and texture collectively define a material state.

children.name: Crystallography
children.description: Lattices, symmetry, planes, directions, and crystal systems.

children.name: Phase Equilibria
children.description: Stable phases, miscibility, order-disorder, and transformations.

children.name: Texture and Anisotropy
children.description: Preferred orientation and direction-dependent properties.

children.name: Defects
children.description: Imperfections that strongly control diffusion, deformation, transformation, transport, and failure.

children.name: Point Defects
children.description: Vacancies, interstitials, substitutional species, charged defects, and defect chemistry.

children.name: Line Defects
children.description: Dislocations, Burgers vectors, slip, and dislocation interactions.

children.name: Planar Defects
children.description: Grain boundaries, stacking faults, twins, and interfaces.

children.name: Volume Defects
children.description: Voids, precipitates, inclusions, second phases, and cracks.

children.name: Thermodynamics, Kinetics, and Transport
children.description: Free energy tells what can happen; kinetics and transport tell how fast and by what path it occurs.

children.name: Thermodynamics
children.description: Chemical potential, free energy, equilibrium, activities, and driving forces.

children.name: Kinetics
children.description: Reaction rates, nucleation, growth, coarsening, and transformation pathways.

children.name: Diffusion
children.description: Atomic mobility in solids, liquids, and along defects or interfaces.

children.name: Heat, Mass, and Momentum Transfer
children.description: Transport foundations for processing, extraction, and coating systems.

children.name: Materials Classes
children.description: The traditional and modern classes of materials studied in curricula and research programs.

children.name: Metallurgy
children.description: Metals and alloys, including extraction, processing, microstructure, deformation, and failure.

children.name: Extractive Metallurgy
children.description: Mineral processing, pyro-, hydro-, and electrometallurgy; refining and recovery.

children.name: Physical Metallurgy
children.description: Phase transformations, diffusion, recovery, recrystallization, grain growth, and heat treatment.

children.name: Mechanical Metallurgy
children.description: Stress, strain, dislocations, work hardening, creep, fatigue, fracture, and failure analysis.

children.name: Ferrous Metallurgy
children.description: Ironmaking, steelmaking, secondary refining, stainless steels, and advanced steels.

children.name: Non-Ferrous Metallurgy
children.description: Aluminum, copper, magnesium, titanium, nickel, and specialty alloy systems.

children.name: Ceramics and Glass
children.description: Bonding-rich inorganic solids, from structural ceramics and refractories to electroceramics and glasses.

children.name: Structural Ceramics
children.description: High hardness, thermal stability, wear resistance, and brittle failure behavior.

children.name: Electronic Ceramics
children.description: Ferroelectrics, dielectrics, piezoelectrics, ionic conductors, and functional oxides.

children.name: Refractories
children.description: High-temperature ceramics for furnaces, reactors, and metallurgical systems.

children.name: Glass and Glass-Ceramics
children.description: Amorphous structure, optical behavior, thermal processing, and controlled crystallization.

children.name: Polymers and Soft Matter
children.description: Macromolecular materials with strong links to chemistry, rheology, viscoelasticity, and processing.

children.name: Polymer Physics
children.description: Chain conformations, transitions, viscoelasticity, and molecular mobility.

children.name: Polymer Processing
children.description: Extrusion, molding, cure, additive manufacturing, and structure development during flow.

children.name: Elastomers and Gels
children.description: Soft, deformable networks and swelling-responsive systems.

children.name: Composites
children.description: Multi-phase materials designed to combine stiffness, strength, toughness, or multifunctionality.

children.name: Polymer Matrix Composites
children.description: Fiber-reinforced lightweight systems common in aerospace and sporting structures.

children.name: Metal Matrix Composites
children.description: Stiff, wear-resistant systems for structural and thermal applications.

children.name: Ceramic Matrix Composites
children.description: High-temperature composite systems with improved damage tolerance.

children.name: Electronic, Magnetic, and Functional Materials
children.description: Materials where electrical, optical, magnetic, or electromechanical response is the main focus.

children.name: Semiconductors
children.description: Band structure, doping, defects, heterostructures, and electronic devices.

children.name: Ferroelectrics and Piezoelectrics
children.description: Polarization switching, domain behavior, and electromechanical coupling.

children.name: Magnetic Materials
children.description: Domains, spin interactions, magnetic anisotropy, and magnetocaloric effects.

children.name: Optoelectronic Materials
children.description: Absorption, emission, excitons, and device-relevant optical response.

children.name: Biomaterials
children.description: Materials for implants, tissue interaction, drug delivery, and biointerfaces.

children.name: Energy Materials
children.description: Battery, solar, thermoelectric, catalyst, fuel-cell, and hydrogen materials.

children.name: Nanomaterials and 2D Materials
children.description: Size-dependent materials, surfaces, nanostructures, and van der Waals systems.

children.name: Synthesis and Processing
children.description: How materials are created, transformed, deposited, densified, joined, and scaled up.

children.name: Casting and Solidification
children.description: Melt processing, segregation, solidification structures, and defects.

children.name: Thermomechanical Processing
children.description: Rolling, forging, extrusion, and texture/microstructure evolution.

children.name: Heat Treatment
children.description: Annealing, quenching, tempering, solution treatment, and aging.

children.name: Powder Processing and Sintering
children.description: Powder metallurgy, compaction, sintering, SPS, hot pressing, and densification.

children.name: Thin Films and Surface Deposition
children.description: Growth and deposition of coatings and thin films for protection and functionality.

children.name: CVD
children.description: Chemical vapor deposition for coatings, 2D materials, semiconductors, and diamond growth.

children.name: PVD
children.description: Physical vapor deposition, sputtering, evaporation, and related vacuum routes.

children.name: ALD
children.description: Atomic layer deposition for conformal, thickness-controlled films.

children.name: PLD and Epitaxy
children.description: Laser-assisted and epitaxial growth routes for crystalline films.

children.name: Solution and Chemical Routes
children.description: Sol-gel, hydrothermal, solvothermal, electrodeposition, and colloidal synthesis.

children.name: Crystal Growth
children.description: Czochralski, Bridgman, vapor transport, flux growth, and specialized crystal-growth methods.

children.name: Additive Manufacturing
children.description: Powder bed fusion, directed energy deposition, binder jetting, and printable materials systems.

children.name: Joining and Surface Engineering
children.description: Welding, brazing, bonding, cladding, surface modification, and protective treatments.

children.name: Characterization and Testing
children.description: How materials are observed, measured, and validated from atomic to component scale.

children.name: Optical Microscopy
children.description: Microstructural inspection, etching contrast, and mesoscale observation.

children.name: X-ray, Neutron, and Synchrotron Methods
children.description: Diffraction, scattering, imaging, local structure, and in situ studies.

children.name: SEM and EBSD
children.description: Surface imaging, fracture analysis, microchemistry, texture, and crystallographic mapping.

children.name: TEM and STEM
children.description: Atomic- and nanoscale imaging, diffraction, spectroscopy, and defects.

children.name: Scanning Probe Methods
children.description: AFM, STM, local electronic structure, and nanoscale surface interactions.

children.name: Spectroscopy
children.description: Raman, FTIR, UV-Vis, PL, XPS, EELS, AES, SIMS, and chemistry-sensitive analysis.

children.name: Thermal Analysis
children.description: DSC, TGA, DMA, dilatometry, and transformation signatures.

children.name: Mechanical Testing
children.description: Tension, compression, hardness, fracture toughness, fatigue, creep, and nanoindentation.

children.name: Electrical, Magnetic, Thermal, and Electrochemical Testing
children.description: Property measurements for devices, batteries, corrosion, and transport.

children.name: In Situ and Operando Characterization
children.description: Watching structure and chemistry evolve during processing or operation.

children.name: Properties and Performance
children.description: What materials do in service and how scientists relate mechanisms to application behavior.

children.name: Mechanical Behavior
children.description: Elasticity, plasticity, work hardening, fatigue, creep, fracture, tribology, and failure.

children.name: Electrical and Electronic Properties
children.description: Conductivity, carrier transport, dielectric response, and switching behavior.

children.name: Thermal Properties
children.description: Heat capacity, thermal conductivity, thermal expansion, and phonon transport.

children.name: Optical and Photonic Properties
children.description: Absorption, emission, refractive index, band gaps, and light-matter interaction.

children.name: Magnetic Properties
children.description: Magnetization, coercivity, anisotropy, spin structure, and magnetotransport.

children.name: Electrochemical and Catalytic Properties
children.description: Redox stability, ionic transport, interfacial kinetics, and catalytic activity.

children.name: Corrosion and Degradation
children.description: Oxidation, aqueous corrosion, SCC, wear, hydrogen damage, and environmental effects.

children.name: Reliability and Lifetime
children.description: Durability, uncertainty, qualification, safe operating windows, and failure prediction.

children.name: Computational Materials
children.description: Simulation, theory, digital design, and multiscale modeling across materials systems.

children.name: Electronic Structure and DFT
children.description: First-principles calculation of bonding, stability, band structure, defects, surfaces, phonons, and response.

children.name: Molecular Dynamics
children.description: Atomistic dynamics, diffusion, thermal transport, interfaces, and deformation at short time scales.

children.name: Monte Carlo and KMC
children.description: Sampling thermodynamics, ordering, diffusion events, and long-time process pathways.

children.name: Phase-Field Modeling
children.description: Mesoscale evolution of grains, precipitates, interfaces, corrosion, fracture, and pattern formation.

children.name: CALPHAD and Computational Thermodynamics
children.description: Thermodynamic databases, multicomponent phase stability, diffusion, and integrated process design.

children.name: Finite Element Modeling
children.description: Stress, heat flow, residual stress, solidification, and component-scale performance.

children.name: ICME and Multiscale Modeling
children.description: Connecting process, structure, properties, and performance across scales.

children.name: Software Ecosystem
children.description: ASE, pymatgen, LAMMPS, VASP-adjacent workflows, KMC engines, and workflow automation.

children.name: Materials Informatics and AI
children.description: Data-driven materials research, machine learning, foundation models, and scientific knowledge systems.

children.name: Data Foundations
children.description: Databases, metadata, curation, APIs, standards, benchmarks, and uncertainty.

children.name: Descriptors and Features
children.description: Composition, structure, defect, image, graph, and Magpie-style descriptors.

children.name: Property Prediction
children.description: Formation energy, stability, band gap, elastic, thermal, electrochemical, and catalytic prediction.

children.name: Microstructure and Process ML
children.description: Processing-structure-property surrogates, process optimization, and inverse design.

children.name: Generative Models for Materials
children.description: Inverse design, crystal generation, molecular generation, and candidate discovery.

children.name: LLMs for Materials Science
children.description: Language models applied to literature mining, scientific reasoning, extraction, RAG, and materials-aware copilots.

children.name: Literature Mining and Synthesis Extraction
children.description: Extracting precursors, processing steps, conditions, and outcomes from papers and patents.

children.name: RAG and Scientific QA
children.description: Retrieval-augmented generation over papers, manuals, and structured databases.

children.name: Domain Fine-Tuning and Benchmarks
children.description: Task-specific adaptation, evaluation sets, and domain-grounded performance measurement.

children.name: CrystaLLM and Structure-Aware Models
children.description: Models that reason over crystal strings, crystal graphs, or structure-conditioned descriptions.

children.name: Ontologies and Knowledge Graphs
children.description: Materials concepts, semantic schemas, ontology-guided extraction, and graph-based knowledge systems.

children.name: Agents and Autonomous Science
children.description: Closed-loop planning, tool use, lab orchestration, and human-in-the-loop scientific copilots.

children.name: Material Families and Communities
children.description: Large research communities often cohere around specific material families or technical ecosystems.

children.name: Steels and Advanced Alloys
children.description: AHSS, stainless steels, superalloys, HEAs/MPEAs, refractory alloys, and design-intensive alloy systems.

children.name: Carbon Materials
children.description: Graphite, diamond, graphene, CNTs, activated carbons, and carbon coatings.

children.name: 2D Materials
children.description: h-BN, TMDs, MXenes, vdW heterostructures, and interface-engineered layered systems.

children.name: Ferroelectrics and HfO2-Based Systems
children.description: Polar oxides, hafnia-based ferroelectrics, domain engineering, and device integration.

children.name: Shape Memory and Smart Materials
children.description: Shape-memory alloys, ferroics, actuators, sensors, and responsive material systems.

children.name: Batteries and Electrochemical Materials
children.description: Cathodes, anodes, solid electrolytes, interfaces, and degradation science.

children.name: Defects Community
children.description: Defect thermodynamics, charged defects, diffusion, irradiation damage, and interface defect science.

children.name: CALPHAD Community
children.description: Phase diagrams, databases, thermodynamic modeling, and computational alloy/process design.

children.name: Applications
children.description: Where materials science is used and how application needs shape design priorities.

children.name: Structural Engineering
children.description: Buildings, infrastructure, tools, transportation, and safety-critical hardware.

children.name: Aerospace Materials
children.description: Lightweight, high-temperature, fatigue-resistant, and multifunctional systems.

children.name: Electronics and Optoelectronics
children.description: Devices, memory, sensors, RF systems, photonics, and packaging.

children.name: Energy Systems
children.description: Batteries, solar, fuel cells, hydrogen, thermoelectrics, catalysts, and nuclear systems.

children.name: Biomedical and Biointerfaces
children.description: Implants, scaffolds, coatings, drug delivery, and biocompatibility.

children.name: Environment and Sustainability
children.description: Carbon capture, membranes, circularity, corrosion mitigation, and critical materials.

children.name: Manufacturing and Digital Twins
children.description: Materials selection, quality control, certification, and digital manufacturing workflows.

children.name: Research Frontiers
children.description: Fast-moving areas where new methods, material systems, and scientific workflows are actively emerging.

children.name: Advanced Synthesis
children.description: CVD, PVD, ALD, epitaxy, autonomous synthesis, and highly controlled growth pathways.

children.name: Interface and Defect Engineering
children.description: Using interfaces and defects as design levers for transport, catalysis, switching, and mechanics.

children.name: Quantum, Spin, and Extreme Materials
children.description: Condensed matter at extremes, superconductivity, topological behavior, scattering, and phonons under extreme conditions.

children.name: Sustainable and Circular Materials
children.description: Low-carbon processing, recyclable systems, critical-mineral substitution, and lifecycle-aware design.

children.name: Digital and Autonomous Laboratories
children.description: Robotics, simulation-in-the-loop, AI planning, active learning, and self-driving materials workflows.

children.name: Foundation Models for Science
children.description: Multimodal scientific models, materials-aware LLMs, and integrated text-structure-image reasoning.
